context: Tests whether the agent performs a thorough code-shape review on a TypeScript module, correctly identifying unsafe type escapes (any, as casts, non-null assertions), dead and duplicate code, catch-all error handling, and comments that only narrate the code — and whether the output follows the expected verdict and findings format.

checklist.name: any type flagged
checklist.description: Flags the use of 'any' type (in decodeToken and/or parseJwt parameters/return types) as a safety failure or type-safety concern

checklist.name: Unsafe cast flagged
checklist.description: Flags the unsafe 'as' cast in refreshToken (casting decoded payload to a specific shape without validation) as a safety concern

checklist.name: Non-null assertion flagged
checklist.description: Flags the non-null assertion operator (payload.userId!) in getUserId as a safety failure

checklist.name: Dead code identified
checklist.description: Identifies legacyValidate as dead/unused code that should be deleted

checklist.name: Duplicate logic identified
checklist.description: Identifies that decodeToken and parseJwt are functionally identical and flags this as duplication

checklist.name: Catch-all error flagged
checklist.description: Flags the generic catch(e) blocks in validateToken and/or hasPermission that swallow all errors without classification

checklist.name: Error classification recommended
checklist.description: Recommends distinguishing between error types (e.g. malformed token, expired token, missing field) rather than collapsing all failures to a single false return

checklist.name: Narrating comments flagged
checklist.description: Identifies at least two comments that merely restate what the code does (e.g. '// Check if the token exists', '// Decode the token', '// Return the user ID field') as commentary that should be removed

checklist.name: Findings tied to impact
checklist.description: At least one finding includes an explanation of what could break or who is affected (e.g. non-null assertion could throw at runtime in production, catch-all silently hides malformed token attacks)

checklist.name: Valid verdict
checklist.description: Report concludes with one of exactly three verdict values: 'ship it', 'needs review', or 'blocked' — no other phrasing
